witnesses.description: Factorization and division witnesses for x^4 - 1 over Q.
A monic GCD witness for x^3 - x and x^2 - 1.

checks.claim: The listed factors multiply to x^4 - 1 over Q.
checks.notes: The validator multiplies the coefficient lists and compares normalized coefficients.

checks.claim: Dividing x^4 - 1 by x - 1 gives x^3 + x^2 + x + 1 with zero remainder.
checks.notes: The validator recomputes polynomial long division and reconstructs divisor*quotient + remainder.

checks.claim: The monic GCD of x^3 - x and x^2 - 1 is x^2 - 1.
checks.notes: The validator recomputes the monic Euclidean GCD and checks the listed quotient divisions.
The validator recomputes p', gcd(p,p'), and the square-free quotient.

checks.claim: The polynomial x^2 + 1 has a factorization into rational linear factors.
checks.notes: The validator recomputes the discriminant and rejects rational linear factors for this fixed quadratic.

checks.claim: General polynomial factorization and irreducibility theory requires a proof-assistant route.

data.target_theorem_shape: Euclidean domains, PIDs/UFDs, primitive part/content lemmas, Gauss lemma, irreducibility criteria, algebraic closure, and complete factorization algorithms over arbitrary fields.
data.future_checker: Lean/mathlib algebra and polynomial libraries plus Axeyum evidence import for fixed finite exact subgoals.

checks.notes: The finite rows are exact rational arithmetic checks; the general theory remains proof-assistant territory.
